name: lemma_specializes_any_n_without_constraint
reason: the lemma instantiates for any n including values far beyond the actual replicas count, so the "pending req or resp in flight" property is claimed for AfterCreatePod(999999) which should never be a reachable reconcile state

name: pending_req_vacuous_when_not_at_state
reason: pending_req_in_flight_or_resp_in_flight_at_reconcile_state is vacuously true whenever the key has no ongoing reconcile, so the "always" invariant in the lemma's precondition is trivially satisfiable for non-existent reconciles — the lemma provides no guarantee when the reconcile is absent

name: unmarshal_unwrap_undefined_on_bad_state
code:
proof fn phi_3_unmarshal_unwrap_undefined_on_bad_state(v1: Value, v2: Value)
    requires
        VReplicaSetReconcileState::unmarshal(v1) is Err,
        VReplicaSetReconcileState::unmarshal(v2) is Err,
    ensures
        at_step_closure(VReplicaSetRecStepView::AfterCreatePod(0))(v1)
            == at_step_closure(VReplicaSetRecStepView::AfterCreatePod(0))(v2),
{
}
reason: unmarshal is uninterpreted so .unwrap() on Err yields an arbitrary but fixed-per-input value — at_step_closure cannot meaningfully distinguish bad states, meaning the reconcile step predicates are undefined on corrupt local state

name: at_step_closure_conflates_steps_on_unmarshal_collision
code:
proof fn phi_4_at_step_closure_conflates_steps_on_unmarshal_collision(v: Value, n1: nat, n2: nat)
    requires
        VReplicaSetReconcileState::unmarshal(v) is Err,
    ensures
        at_step_closure(VReplicaSetRecStepView::AfterCreatePod(n1))(v)
            == at_step_closure(VReplicaSetRecStepView::AfterCreatePod(n2))(v),
{
}
reason: when unmarshal fails, unwrap produces the same arbitrary value regardless of which step is being tested — all step predicates collapse to the same boolean, making step-specific reasoning unsound on non-unmarshallable states

name: object_ref_ignores_uid
code:
proof fn phi_5_object_ref_ignores_uid(v1: VReplicaSetView, v2: VReplicaSetView)
    requires
        v1.metadata.name is Some,
        v2.metadata.name is Some,
        v1.metadata.namespace is Some,
        v2.metadata.namespace is Some,
        v1.metadata.name == v2.metadata.name,
        v1.metadata.namespace == v2.metadata.namespace,
        v1.metadata.uid != v2.metadata.uid,
    ensures
        v1.object_ref() == v2.object_ref(),
{
}
reason: object_ref uses only kind/name/namespace and ignores uid, so a deleted-and-recreated VReplicaSet with a new uid maps to the same ObjectRef — ongoing_reconciles keyed by object_ref could conflate old and new resource instances
